steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage opens by describing what the Federalist Papers are, then pivots with 'Though' to focus on authorship — what is certain and what is uncertain. The correct choice must capture both the collection's description and the authorship complexity.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: focuses only on the arguments for ratification and omits any mention of authorship uncertainty. ✗
  • Choice B: describes the collection (85 essays by Hamilton, Jay, and Madison, published 1787–88, arguing for ratification) and addresses authorship (most essays have certain attribution, but some like No. 52 may be Hamilton's or Madison's). ✓
  • Choice C: centers on publication history rather than authorship. ✗
  • Choice D: misrepresents authorship as collaborative multi-author writing rather than uncertain single authorship. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
